IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: MOBILIZING THE POWER OF COMMUNITY AND THE RESOURCES OF PHILANTHROPY TO ADVANCE EQUITY FOR PEOPLE, PLACES AND THE PLANET.
IRS990.MissionDesc: MOBILIZING THE POWER OF COMMUNITY AND THE RESOURCES OF PHILANTHROPY TO ADVANCE EQUITY FOR PEOPLE, PLACES AND THE PLANET.
IRS990.Desc: IN FISCAL YEAR 2024, MARIN COMMUNITY FOUNDATION (MCF) GRANTED OVER $200 MILLION FROM ITS FAMILY AND COMMUNITY FUNDS TO NONPROFITS IN MARIN COUNTY, ELSEWHERE IN THE U.S., AND AROUND THE WORLD. THESE GRANTS SUPPORTED A WIDE RANGE OF COMMUNITY NEEDS, INCLUDING SOCIAL SERVICES, EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS, THE ARTS AND ENVIRONMENTAL ISSUES.

OtherLiabilitiesOrgGrp.Desc: AMOUNTS DUE TO BENEFICIARIES OF SPLIT-INTEREST AGREEMENTS
AMOUNTS INVESTED ON BEHALF OF NONPROFIT ORGANIZATIONS

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE MARIN COMMUNITY FOUNDATION (MCF) HOLDS ASSETS IN DONOR-RESTRICTED ENDOWMENT FUNDS IN ORDER TO CARRY OUT ITS MISSION OF ENCOURAGING AND APPLYING PHILANTHROPIC CONTRIBUTIONS.

GrantsToOrgOutsideUSGrp.PurposeOfGrantTxt: SUPPORT CONSERVATION OF MARINE BIODIVERSITY AND ESTABLISHMENT OF MARINE PROTECTED AREAS CONTRIBUTING TO LOCAL COMMUNITIES AND TRADITIONAL CULTURE IN AMAMI OSHIMA ISLAND, JAPAN

PROVIDE SUPPORT FOR BETTER MANAGEMENT OF SCOTLANDS INSHORE WATERS, INCLUDING AN INSHORE LIMIT BAN ON BOTTOM DRAGGED FISHING GEAR WITHIN THREE MILES OF THE COAST, PREFERENTIAL ACCESS TO FISHERIES FOR BOATS WITH LOW IMPACT FISHING METHODS, AND VESSEL MON

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: MARIN COMMUNITY FOUNDATION FOLLOWS THE SAME PROCEDURES FOR ALL OF ITS GRANTS AS DESCRIBED IN SCHEDULE I, PART IV. IN ADDITION, THE FOUNDATION FOLLOWS THE EXPENDITURE RESPONSIBILITY (ER) OR EQUIVALENCY DETERMINATION RULES (ED) FOR ALL OF ITS FOREIGN GRANTS. FOR BOTH ED AND ER GRANTS, THE GRANTEE SIGNS A GRANT AGREEMENT CONFIRMING THAT THE FUNDS WILL BE USED IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE PURPOSE AND IRS REGULATIONS ARTICULATED IN THE DOCUMENTS. FOR ER GRANTS, THE GRANTEES PROVIDE A NARRATIVE AND AN EXPENDITURE REPORT DETAILING THE USE OF THE FUNDS AS FORMERLY AGREED TO IN THE GRANT AGREEMENT.
REGION: CENTRAL AMERICA AND THE CARIBBEAN (F) TOTAL EXPENDITURES FOR AND INVESTMENTS IN THE REGION: $132,231,245
LINE 3A, SUBTOTAL: $166,750,143 LINE 3C, TOTALS (ADD LINES 3A AND 3B): $166,750,143

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: TO QUALIFY FOR A GRANT DISTRIBUTION FROM THE FOUNDATION A GRANTEE MUST BE ABLE TO SATISFY THE FOUNDATION'S DUE DILIGENCE REQUIREMENTS. THIS PROCESS INCLUDES MAKING AN INDEPENDENT INVESTIGATION OF THE PROSPECTIVE GRANTEE TO ESTABLISH THAT IT QUALIFIES TO RECEIVE THE GRANT. THE GRANTEE ORGANIZATION MUST ALSO HAVE THE CAPACITY TO FULFILL THE TERMS OF THE GRANT AND BE WILLING TO FURNISH THE FOUNDATION WITH ANY REQUIRED EVALUATIVE REPORTS. FOR GRANTS ORIGINATING FROM DONOR-ADVISED FUNDS, EACH GRANTEE IS INSTRUCTED IN WRITING THAT NO ONE ASSOCIATED WITH THE FUND MAY RECEIVE ANY PERSONAL BENEFIT BECAUSE OF THE GRANT, AND THAT THE GRANT MUST BE USED FOR CHARITABLE PURPOSES ONLY. DEPENDING UPON THE SIZE AND SOURCE OF THE GRANT, A WRITTEN REPORT FROM THE GRANTEE MAY BE REQUIRED.
MARIN COMMUNITY FOUNDATION PROVIDES A REIMBURSEMENT OF UP TO $600 EACH CALENDAR YEAR FOR QUALIFIED HEALTH AND FITNESS EXPENSES TO ALL EMPLOYEES.
DURING THE 2023 CALENDAR YEAR, VIKKI GARROD RECEIVED A NON-FIXED BONUS OF $1,448 FOR PERFORMING EXTRA JOB DUTIES DURING THE YEAR.
THIS NUMBER REPRESENTS THE NUMBER OF CONTRIBUTIONS MADE NOT THE NUMBER OF ITEMS CONTRIBUTED.

MCF SUPPORTED THE PHILANTHROPIC INVOLVEMENT OF INDIVIDUALS, FAMILIES, BUSINESSES, AND ORGANIZATIONS IN THE COMMUNITY THROUGH THE ESTABLISHMENT OF UNRESTRICTED, DONOR-ADVISED, FIELD-OF-INTEREST, DESIGNATED BENEFICIARY, AND SCHOLARSHIP FUNDS. AT THE END OF FISCAL YEAR 2024, MCF ADMINISTERED OVER 850 COMPONENT FUNDS AND 9 ACTIVE SUPPORTING ORGANIZATIONS. II. DISTRIBUTION TRUSTEE OF THE LEONARD AND BERYL BUCK TRUST ("BUCK FAMILY FUND"): MCF IS ALSO THE DISTRIBUTION TRUSTEE FOR THE LEONARD AND BERYL BUCK FOUNDATION, A CHARITABLE TRUST ESTABLISHED IN 1979 TO DISTRIBUTE FUNDS FOR NON-PROFIT CHARITABLE, RELIGIOUS, OR EDUCATIONAL ACTIVITIES IN MARIN COUNTY, CALIFORNIA. III. LOANS AT THE END OF FY 2024, THE COMMUNITY INVESTMENT LOAN FUND HAD 22 LOANS IN ITS PORTFOLIO AND $17.2 MILLION IN PRINCIPAL OUTSTANDING. THE FOUNDATION MAKES BELOW-MARKET RATE LOANS TO NONPROFIT ORGANIZATIONS TO MEET A NUMBER OF OBJECTIVES: TO PROVIDE INVESTMENT CAPITAL PROJECTS AND REVENUE-PRODUCING VENTURES; TO ENCOURAGE THE ECONOMIC INDEPENDENCE AND STABILITY OF NONPROFIT ORGANIZATIONS; TO PROVIDE ALTERNATIVES TO OR AUGMENT TRADITIONAL FUNDING SOURCES; TO MEET EMERGENCY CASH FLOW NEEDS AND OTHER SITUATIONS; AND TO FINANCE EQUIPMENT PURCHASES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: FORM 990 IS PROVIDED TO THE CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER FOR REVIEW BEFORE FILING.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ALL MEMBERS OF THE GOVERNING BOARD, OFFICERS AND KEY EMPLOYEES MUST REVIEW THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY AND COMPLETE A QUESTIONNAIRE CONCERNING COMPLIANCE ON AN ANNUAL BASIS. THE FORMS ARE REVIEWED BY THE CORPORATE SECRETARY AND CFO TO ENSURE ONGOING COMPLIANCE AND DETERMINE WHETHER A CONFLICT EXISTS. ALL CONFLICTS AND POTENTIAL CONFLICTS ARE REQUIRED TO BE DISCLOSED, AND THE PERSON IS EXCLUDED FROM PARTICIPATING IN DELIBERATIONS AND DECISIONS CONCERNING THE MATTER.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE PRESIDENT AND CEO IS THE TOP MANAGEMENT OFFICIAL OF THE ORGANIZATION AND THEIR COMPENSATION PACKAGE IS REVIEWED AND DECIDED UPON ANNUALLY BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS. A COMPENSATION COMMITTEE COMPRISED OF SEVERAL INDEPENDENT MEMBERS OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS IS ESTABLISHED ANNUALLY. AS PART OF THIS PROCESS, THE COMPENSATION COMMITTEE PREPARES A WRITTEN PERFORMANCE APPRAISAL WITH INPUT FROM ALL BOARD MEMBERS. FURTHER, THE COMMITTEE COMPARES THE PRESIDENT'S COMPENSATION TO THAT OF OTHERS IN SIMILAR POSITIONS USING VARIOUS SURVEYS. EVERY FEW YEARS, THE COMMITTEE ENGAGES AN INDEPENDENT EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION CONSULTANT TO EVALUATE THE PRESIDENT'S COMPENSATION PACKAGE TO DETERMINE IF THE COMPONENTS, AS WELL AS, THE PACKAGE AS A WHOLE CONTINUE TO BE FAIR AND REASONABLE. DURING EXECUTIVE SESSION AT THE END OF THE FISCAL YEAR, WITH ALL BOARD MEMBERS ATTENDING, THE COMMITTEE REPORTS THEIR FINDINGS, THE OVERALL APPRAISAL, AND THEIR RECOMMENDATION. AFTER REVIEW AND DISCUSSION, THE BOARD DETERMINES THE PRESIDENT AND CEO'S COMPENSATION FOR THE NEXT YEAR. A SIMILAR PROCESS IS USED TO REVIEW THE CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICERS' COMPENSATION. SALARY ADJUSTMENTS FOR ALL OTHER STAFF, INCLUDING OTHER OFFICERS, ARE APPROVED BY THE PRESIDENT AND CEO WITHIN THE BUDGETARY GUIDELINES APPROVED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS. EVERY FEW YEARS AN OUTSIDE CONSULTANT IS ENGAGED TO REVIEW INDUSTRY AND REGIONAL SURVEYS TO ENSURE ALL STAFF MEMBERS' COMPENSATION LEVELS ARE FAIR AND REASONABLE. THE LAST SUCH REVIEW WAS COMPLETED IN MAY 2023.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: GOVERNING DOCUMENTS AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS ARE AVAILABLE TO PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.
